title: Illinois Increases Biodiesel Blend Rate to B17
content: A bipartisan bill passed in 2022 to promote the use of higher biodiesel blends is making a significant impact once again in 2025. Effective today, April 1, biodiesel blend requirements will increase from B14 to B17, marking another step toward advancing cleaner fuel options. Illinois Soybean Association (ISA) Chairman and Illinois soybean farmer Ron Kindred is proud to see the continuation of efforts that support our stateâ€™s farmers. â€œIncreasing biodiesel blend rates is a win for farmers, consumers and the environment,â€ said Kindred. In 2026, the minimum biodiesel blend levels eligible for a tax exemption jump to 20%.
author: Analysts Account
